description: Speculation swirled online after a minor road incident involving a vehicle linked to actor Darshan Thoogudeepa's son, Vineesh. Contrary to unverified reports of an accident and hospitalization, Darshan's wife, Vijayalakshmi, clarified that Vineesh was safe and not driving. She stated their driver was at the wheel during the small mishap, with the other party admitting fault. The family urged for fact-checking to prevent further distress.

'Welcome to family bhabhi': Ketan Agarwal-Siya Goyal's pre-wedding video surfaces
description: A pre-wedding video showcasing realtor Ketan Agarwal and his fiancée Siya Goyal's apparent happiness has emerged, just days after police accused Goyal and her alleged lover, Chetan Chaudhary, of conspiring to murder the 26-year-old. Ketan died at Lohagad Fort, initially ruled an accident, but investigations revealed extensive communication between Goyal and Chaudhary, allegedly planning his death. The couple is now in custody.

Rain threat looms over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's likely India debut
description: India's T20I opener against Ireland in Belfast faces potential rain disruptions, though conditions are expected to clear by the evening's scheduled start. All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who could debut after a stellar IPL season. This series also marks Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut for India in the T20 format.

description: Accused Siya Goyal's mother, Pooja Goyal, on Thursday demanded stringent punishment for her daughter in connection with the alleged murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal, asserting that if her daughter is found guilty of the crime, she should face the same fate and be thrown from the exact spot where the victim was dumped.

'Submerged her head in bucket': 6-month-pregnant woman killed by husband in Faridabad
description: A shocking incident unfolded in Faridabad where a six-month pregnant woman, Neha Kumari, was allegedly murdered by her husband, Amit Gupta, following a domestic dispute. Police reported that Gupta assaulted his wife and then drowned her in a bucket of water before fleeing. An FIR has been filed, and authorities are actively searching for the absconding husband.

Delhi 10-year-old rape-murder: Cabbie waited 45 mins for asleep girl to separate from mother
description: A cabbie accused of rape and murder stalked his young victim for nearly an hour before abducting her from a south Delhi street. Police are awaiting forensic reports from hair strands found in the cab and at the dumping site, along with a potency test on the accused. The accused's cab ID has been blocked as investigators delve into his background and a recent road accident.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
description: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

description: A shocking murder case involving realtor Ketan Agarwal has taken a dramatic turn as his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her friend, Chetan Chaudhary, reportedly blame each other for his death. Police investigations reveal conflicting accounts, with evidence currently pointing towards Goyal as the alleged mastermind behind the plot. The couple was set to marry later this year.

'Blood-stained axe, cutter, burnt face': Couple found dead inside car in Madhya Pradesh
description: A young couple, Ritik Soni and Muskan Jain, were discovered deceased in a car near Ashoknagar, Madhya Pradesh. Police found a pistol, bloodied axe, and cutter at the scene, investigating possibilities of murder and suicide. The families allege a pre-planned murder, citing suspicious circumstances including a partially burnt face and a fire in the car. The couple reportedly knew each other for years and had worked together previously.

title: Afghan among 7 held as cross-border arms, drugs network unearthed; 5-kg heroin, 10 pistols seized

Cash, gold, diamonds and flats: Suspended Shamirpet tahsildar under ACB scanner over Rs 20cr assets
description: Anti-Corruption Bureau sleuths unearthed assets worth an estimated Rs 20 crore during a fresh probe into a revenue official already arrested for bribery. Searches at four locations revealed properties, cash, jewellery, and bank deposits disproportionate to her known income. The tahsildar, T Sucharita, was previously arrested in May for demanding and accepting a bribe for an official favour and is currently in judicial remand.

title: Lucknow building fire: Artist’s anniversary sketch for parents reaches them after his death
description: A poignant sketch of parents, intended as a wedding anniversary gift, became a heartbreaking memento for Shatruhan Lal and Santosh Kumari after their 24-year-old son, 3D artist Nilesh Kumar, perished in the Aliganj fire. The drawing, a testament to his affection, was discovered among his belongings, a silent yet powerful final message of love.

Day after tehrvi, ‘dead’ Ghaziabad man returns home
description: A Ghaziabad family mourned and cremated Girdhar Bisht, believing him murdered, only for him to return home 13 days later. Bisht, who had been in jail and then went missing, claims he was meditating in Punjab. His reappearance has baffled neighbours and police, who are now tasked with identifying the body they cremated and investigating Bisht's erratic past and alleged disputes.

'Is Rs 100 worth of our dead’: Mathura villagers ask after JCB damages 9 graves
description: In Mathura, the Kabristan Ahle Muslimeen committee is livid about the government's decision to provide only Rs 100 as compensation for each of the nine graves reportedly wrecked by a JCB. This minimal relief has been deemed disrespectful by grieving families who directly witnessed the vandalism. Locals claim that despite official assurances of compliance with protocols, there is a striking lack of empathy related to waqf land issues.

'Very fishy': Mumbai mayor suspects foul play in BMC staffer’s fall into flooded open drain
description: Mayor Ritu Tawde has ordered a thorough investigation into a BMC staffer's fall into an open drain during her inspection of a flooded market. She finds the incident suspicious, questioning how a civic employee could fall into a known hazard when others passed safely. The staffer, Baban Jadhav, denies any wrongdoing, stating he was merely performing his duties. Officials are examining safety protocols and the circumstances surrounding the fall.

title: Lost money to cyberfraud? 2 things to do immediately and 1 mistake to avoid
description: Prime Minister Modi has urged states to expedite e-Zero FIRs for cyber fraud victims, aiming to streamline complaint registration and bypass jurisdictional hurdles. This push comes amid a surge in organized cybercrime, highlighted by a recent CBI crackdown. The article emphasizes the critical first hour after a fraud, advising immediate calls to the 1930 helpline and online reporting to maximize fund recovery chances.
